refinement_sections.content: The logistics company wants to improve their package sorting process. Currently, they use a simple algorithm that only sorts by one factor, which isn't very efficient. They have data on package size, weight, and priority, but they haven’t said how to use this data for sorting. Also, they want to reduce the time packages take in sorting, but they haven’t explained what that means exactly.

I need to figure out what factors to use for sorting. Since they gave me dimensions, weight, and priority, I should consider how each of these could affect sorting speed. For example, maybe heavier packages should be sorted differently than lighter ones, or maybe priority packages should be sorted first.

The company wants to minimize the time packages spend in sorting, but I need to define what that means. Does it mean the total time taken for all packages, or the average time per package? I should probably ask how they measure time in their current system.
I will have to design a new algorithm that uses the chosen sorting criteria. I might think about a multi-attribute sorting method that takes into account more than one factor at a time.

Since I don’t have all the details, I have to make some assumptions. For example, I might assume that priority packages are the most important and should be sorted first. I also need to assume how the different attributes will interact in the sorting process.

I will need to think about how my new algorithm could be better than the current one. This could involve estimating how much faster packages will be sorted with my method compared to the current one, but I will need to be careful because I don’t have all the data.
